title: What Is Hunter Biden Doing?
description: The former president’s son tearfully met with Candace Owens, who once called him a “degenerate.”
author: Matt Viser

title: Tulsi Gabbard Takes the Exit Ramp
description: Excluded from Trump’s inner circle, she sought the president’s approval by spreading baseless claims.
author: Shane Harris

title: The Ahmadinejad Option
description: The idea that Israel and the United States might back Mahmoud Ahmadinejad in a coup has drawn guffaws from several different groups.
author: Graeme Wood

title: How Trump’s Culture War Derailed a New Smithsonian Museum
description: Republicans wanted to narrow the scope of the Women’s History Museum and give the president power over its location. The attempt failed.
author: Kelsey Ables

title: The Sheep DetectivesIs More Than Just Fluff
description: Its cast of talking animals solve mysteries and move audiences to tears.
author: Shirley Li

title: The Surprising Lesson of theGrantaControversy
description: Literary organizations might need to get more familiar with AI if they want to protect against its encroachment.
author: Boris Kachka

title: How Dropout Cracked Internet Comedy
description: Sam Reich on building “better internet” and not “worse Netflix”
author: Charlie Warzel

title: Israel’s Human Wrecking Ball
description: And the prime minister who empowers him
author: Avi Issacharoff

title: Ken Paxton Is Actually Doing This
description: The Texas Senate primary exists in an alternate reality.
author: Elaine Godfrey

title: Photos of the Week: Chief Mouser, Pet Gala, Soapbox Race
description: A scene from the 79th annual Cannes Film Festival, wildfires in Southern California, a bison in Yellowstone National Park, a dust storm in Iraq, a flower show in London, and much more
author: Alan Taylor

title: The Goodbye Stephen Colbert Wanted to Say
description: The late-night host ended his talk show the way he started it—with empathy, and an eye for entertainment.
author: David Sims

title: Donald Trump’s Paint Jobs
description: With his changes to the Reflecting Pool and other sites, the president is asking Washingtonians to trust his taste.

title: What the Trump Administration Needs to Do to Contain the Ebola Outbreak
description: We’re already playing from behind, but it’s not too late to limit the damage.
author: Stephanie Psaki,Beth Cameron,and Jon Finer

title: The Economic Experiment That Upended Reality
description: Minimum-wage increases were expected to kill jobs. The fact that they didn’t should make us rethink a lot of assumptions.
author: Nick Hanauerand Eric Beinhocker

title: Two Futures for the American Left
description: The dustup over Marjorie Taylor Greene isn’t really about tactical coalitions. It’s about values.
author: Arash Azizi

title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: America’s Centennial
description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.

title: What’s Behind the New Trump Child-Savings Accounts
description: The policy has bipartisan appeal—but there are factors that could inhibit its success.

title: A Close Reading of Trump’s Statement on Attending His Son’s Wedding
author: Jonathan Chait

title: Trump’s Endgame Is Surrender
description: He seems to hope to slip away without Americans noticing the magnitude of this defeat.

title: Will Americans Ever Lose Their Grip on the Handshake?
description: This default greeting isn’t always as friendly and egalitarian as it seems.
author: Tom Bartlett
